title: Two legitimate meanings of square
role: Hold repeated scaling and area scaling side by side without collapsing them.
prompt_core: Create a two-panel image. One panel shows a scale factor acting twice along a one-dimensional line of transformations; the other shows a unit square growing into a larger square whose area changes in two directions at once. The panels should echo each other so the learner feels that both are legitimate meanings of square in different roles. Architectural clarity, tactile surfaces, adult elegance.
caption: Squaring can mean repeated scaling or two-direction area growth.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "9.1 Two legitimate meanings of square".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Hold repeated scaling and area scaling side by side without collapsing them.

Layout intent:
S — Split-screen corrective plate

Style direction:
dual-meaning square plate

Core image brief:
Create a two-panel image. One panel shows a scale factor acting twice along a one-dimensional line of transformations; the other shows a unit square growing into a larger square whose area changes in two directions at once. The panels should echo each other so the learner feels that both are legitimate meanings of square in different roles. Architectural clarity, tactile surfaces, adult elegance.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
Squaring can mean repeated scaling or two-direction area growth.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: Why squares appear everywhere
role: Connect squares to independent directions and invariants.
prompt_core: Show several contexts where two independent directions matter: a square floor tile, a velocity component diagram, a variance cloud, and a Pythagorean right triangle, all quietly linked by a luminous x-squared pattern of growth in two directions. The image should feel like a conceptual family portrait explaining why squares keep returning throughout mathematics and physics.
caption: Squares are the signature of two independent directions combining.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

source_key: 9.3 Square root as an inverse question
title: Square root as an inverse question
role: Make root-finding feel like asking for the side that would produce a given square.
prompt_core: Illustrate a known area as a luminous square field or tiled surface, while an unknown side length is being recovered from it like the unlocking dimension of the figure. The scene should feel like de-compression: a two-direction effect being resolved back into one length-like cause. Calm, architectural, with a sense of inverse restoration rather than trick solving.
caption: A square root asks for the side whose square would produce the given whole.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. title: Why negatives vanish under squaring
role: Reinforce that squaring discards directional information.
prompt_core: Show two equal-magnitude directed segments pointing opposite ways feeding into the same resulting square or area block. The transformation should visibly forget the left-right distinction and keep only magnitude. Clean symmetry, muted palette, highly legible composition.
caption: Opposite directions can generate the same square because orientation is lost.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

source_key: 9.5 Historical note: the diagonal of the square
title: Historical note: the diagonal of the square
role: Historicize the diagonal as the doorway to irrationality and geometry’s authority.
prompt_core: Create a quiet but charged image of a square floor tile or drawn square with its diagonal drawn across it, surrounded by measuring cords that never settle into a common exact repeat. Let the diagonal feel almost like a blade opening a crack in the old arithmetic worldview. Stone, chalk, dusk light, seriousness without melodrama.
caption: The diagonal forced mathematics to admit lengths beyond fraction.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite felt experiments with area growth and inverse recovery.
prompt_core: Show a small set of tiles growing from a 1-by-1 square to a 2-by-2 and 3-by-3 arrangement, then a separate unlabeled square area asking what side could have made it. Minimal, lucid, almost like a note in the margin of an architect’s notebook. The image should trigger mental calculation by structure, not formula.
caption: Grow the square, then ask what side the area is hiding.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles.
